title: AltimateAI/altimate-code
description: Open-source agentic data engineering harness for dbt, SQL, and cloud warehouses. 100+ tools, 10 warehouses, AI-powered.

title: bytedance/deer-flow
description: An open-source long-horizon SuperAgent harness that researches, codes, and creates. With the help of sandboxes, memories, tools, skill, subagents and message gateway, it handles different levels of tasks that could take minutes to hours.

title: can1357/oh-my-pi
description: ⌥  AI Coding agent for the terminal — hash-anchored edits, optimized tool harness, LSP, Python, browser, subagents, and more

title: first-fluke/oh-my-agent
description: Portable multi-agent harness for .agents-based skills, workflows, and standards-aware agent teams across Antigravity, Claude Code, Codex, Cursor, OpenCode, and more.

title: OpenSource03/harnss
description: Open-source, desktop client/UI build to harness Claude Code, Codex and any other Agent accepting Agent Client Protocol. Run multiple AI coding agents side by side with rich tool visualization, MCP integrations, built-in terminal, git, browser and just about anything else you may need.

title: agent-sh/agnix
description: The missing linter and lsp for AI coding assistants. Validate CLAUDE.md, AGENTS.md, SKILL.md, hooks, MCP. Plugin for all major IDEs included, with autofixes.

authors: Masaki Shiraishi, Ikko Hamamura, Tatsuya Ishigaki

title: RemoteAgent: Bridging Vague Human Intents and Earth Observation with RL-based Agentic MLLMs
description: Earth Observation (EO) systems are essentially designed to support domain experts who often express their requirements through vague natural language rather than precise, machine-friendly instructions. authors: Liang Yao, Shengxiang Xu, Fan Liu

title: Multi-Agent Orchestration for High-Throughput Materials Screening on a Leadership-Class System
description: The integration of Artificial Intelligence (AI) with High-Performance Computing (HPC) is transforming scientific workflows from human-directed pipelines into adaptive systems capable of autonomous decision-making. authors: Thang Duc Pham, Harikrishna Tummalapalli, Fakhrul Hasan Bhuiyan
